_comment: Committed REGRESSION FLOORS per dataset AND per backend. Schema: datasets[dataset][backend]. These are NOT a quality claim for the mock: the keyword mock scores POORLY on realistic resumes (coordinator) on purpose - that gap is why the Claude engine exists. CI runs `eval_accuracy.py --all --check` (mock) and FAILS if a change pushes a metric past a floor. Floors carry slack for cross-version (scipy) jitter; tighten/loosen DELIBERATELY. To ADD real-engine baselines, run on a box with the CLI + CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: `python scripts/eval_accuracy.py --backend claude_cli --runs 3 --write-baseline` (measures with variance and merges datasets[coordinator][claude_cli] here); commit the result. Mock measured 2026-06-21: coordinator acc 0.25 / within1 0.542 / spearman -0.097 / mae 47.9; labeled acc 1.0 / within1 1.0 / spearman 0.975 / mae 8.61.

mock.role: clear-cut seed; pipeline smoke/sanity only (NOT real-world accuracy)
